mainmatter.p: A category with zero morphisms is defined to be a category enriched in the category of pointed sets and strict maps. The distinguished point of \(\mathbf{hom}_{\mathcal{A}}{\left(A,B\right)}\) shall be written \(0\).
Let \({\left(V,I,\otimes,\alpha,\lambda,\rho\right)}\) be a monoidal category. A category \(C\) enriched in \(V\) is defined by the following structure and axioms:

ol.li: for any two objects \(x,y\), an object \(\mathbf{hom}_{C}{\left(x,y\right)}\in V\);
for every object \(x\), a morphism \(I\xrightarrow{i_{x}}\mathbf{hom}_{C}{\left(x,x\right)}\) in \(V\);
for all objects \(x,y,z\), a morphism \(\mathbf{hom}_{C}{\left(x,y\right)}\otimes\mathbf{hom}_{C}{\left(y,z\right)}\xrightarrow{c_{x,y,z}}\mathbf{hom}_{C}{\left(x,z\right)}\) in \(V\).
such that the following diagrams commute in \(V\):

mainmatter.p: Evidently, the kernel of a morphism \(A\xrightarrow{f} B\) in a category with zero morphisms is the equalizer of \(A\xrightarrow{f} B\) with the zero morphism \(A\xrightarrow{0} B\).
We will often write \(\operatorname{ker}{f}\xrightarrow{i_{\operatorname{ker}{f}}}A\) for the kernel of a map \(A\xrightarrow{f}B\) in a category with zero morphisms \(\mathcal{A}\).

Let \(\mathcal{A}\) be a category with zero morphisms. Then the kernel of a morphism \(A\xrightarrow{f} B\) in \(\mathcal{A}\) is defined to be a morphism \(K\xrightarrow{k}{A}\) such that for any \(X\xrightarrow{h}A\) with \(h;f=0\), there exists a unique map \(X\xrightarrow{}K\)factoring \(X\xrightarrow{h}A\) through \(K\xrightarrow{k}{A}\) in the sense of the diagram below:
